title: e2e test: fixed 3s sleep for connection readiness is load-sensitive
description: cli/tests/e2e_payment.rs line 216: tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(3)) is the only synchronization between subprocess spawn and the /pay command. On a loaded CI machine, 3 seconds may not be enough for both alice and bob subprocesses to start, TCP-connect to the relay, complete the auth handshake, and exchange DirectoryList events — meaning Alice sends /pay before the MLS group is established and the command is silently dropped. The test then times out with no indication of whether the payment was attempted. A more robust approach: poll Bob's stdout for a line indicating both clients are in the group (e.g., '[MLS] group active'), or use a relay-side 'DirectoryList' containing both clients as the readiness signal. The fixed sleep is a latent source of flakiness that will surface in CI.
